summary: Unmerged tooling PR proposing helper scripts for using Transmuter against Melee single translation units. It added a prepare script to generate/copy a `.ctx` file to `.ctx.c` and print a `transmuter match` command, plus a compile wrapper that invokes the repo's MWCC/SJIS toolchain with Melee-like CFLAGS. The PR also intentionally reordered two calls in `ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds` to create a mismatch demo. It was closed after feedback asking for resubmission in a more complete state with clearer usage documentation.
what_changed: Added `tools/transmuter-prepare.sh`, a Bash helper that accepts `<src/path/to/file.c> <function_name>`, checks that the source is under `src/`, maps it to `build/GALE01/...`, runs `ninja` for the `.ctx`, copies it to `.ctx.c`, validates the target object path, locates a Transmuter CLI path, and prints a suggested `node ... match` command.

Added `tools/transmuter-compile.sh`, a Bash compile wrapper for Transmuter that accepts `<input.c> <output.o>`, expects repo root as CWD, chooses `wibo` on Linux when available or falls back to `wine`, and runs `sjiswrap.exe` plus `mwcceppc.exe` using a hardcoded `GC/1.2.5n` MWCC version and Melee-style C flags/includes.

Modified `src/melee/if/iftime.c` inside `ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds` by swapping the order of `gm_8016AF0C()` and `gm_8016AEEC()` assignment calls. The PR body says this was purposely done to break the function as a Transmuter demo, not as a production code change.

Documented a sample Transmuter invocation in the PR body using `build/GALE01/src/melee/if/iftime.ctx.c`, target object `build/GALE01/obj/melee/if/iftime.o`, `--function ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds`, the compile wrapper, `--cwd`, `--isolate`, `--no-reduce`, `--no-cleanup`, concurrency, timeout, iteration limit, and seed.
smart_moves: The compile wrapper tried to mirror the existing `mwcc_sjis` ninja rule rather than inventing a separate compile environment, which is the right direction for matching-sensitive tooling.
The prepare script automated the path mapping from `src/.../*.c` to `build/GALE01/.../*.ctx`, `*.ctx.c`, and `build/GALE01/obj/.../*.o`, reducing manual mistakes when setting up Transmuter runs.
Copying `.ctx` to `.ctx.c` addressed extension expectations from both MWCC and Transmuter while preserving the original generated `.ctx` for reuse.
The helper surfaced `--isolate` as a default tactic and documented that it strips non-target/non-inline bodies and may reduce compile/search work.
The wrapper included practical platform handling for `wibo` versus `wine`, matching the kind of host-environment friction common in Melee decomp tooling.
decomp_lessons: Matching-search tools need a compile wrapper that is as close as possible to the project's real compiler invocation; mismatched CFLAGS, include paths, MWCC version, or wrapper selection can invalidate search results.
For single-function mutation/search workflows, first generate a preprocessed ctx-style source from ninja, then target the extracted object from the normal build tree.
Demo PRs that intentionally break matched source should clearly separate demonstration-only perturbations from intended project changes, or avoid committing the broken source at all.
Tooling additions should include complete, maintainable usage documentation before review; this PR was closed specifically for being incomplete and unclear.
Environment assumptions such as Transmuter checkout location, `TRANSMUTER` override, `wibo`/`wine`, and required prior `ninja` build should be explicit in docs or scripts.
naming_conventions: Helper scripts used the `tools/transmuter-*.sh` naming pattern: `transmuter-prepare.sh` for setup and `transmuter-compile.sh` for compiler integration.
Generated ctx copy convention in the script was `build/GALE01/<src-without-.c>.ctx.c`, derived from ninja's `build/GALE01/<src-without-.c>.ctx`.
Target object path convention was `build/GALE01/obj/${SRC#src/}` with `.c` replaced by `.o`, e.g. `src/melee/if/iftime.c` to `build/GALE01/obj/melee/if/iftime.o`.
The demonstration function retained existing project symbol casing: `ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds`, `ifTime_ShowTimers`, `gm_8016AF0C`, and `gm_8016AEEC`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `ninja <build/GALE01/...>.ctx` to generate the preprocessed translation unit for the source file under investigation.
Feed Transmuter a `.ctx.c` copy of the generated ctx source so the compiler and Transmuter accept the input extension.
Use `--target <object.o>` and `--function <function_name>` to constrain Transmuter to the target function/object.
Pass a compiler command template like `tools/transmuter-compile.sh {{inputPath}} {{outputPath}}` so Transmuter can compile candidate sources with the repo's MWCC setup.
Run Transmuter with `--cwd "$(pwd)"` from the repo root so include paths and relative tool paths resolve.

Consider `--isolate` to strip non-target, non-inline function bodies from the ctx before search. The script comments claim this can make compiles modestly faster and reduce iterations, but the PR provides only anecdotal evidence.
The example used bounded search parameters such as `--concurrency 4`, `--max-iterations 2000`, `--timeout 180000`, and optionally `--seed 42` for reproducibility.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Please resubmit this when it's in a more complete state and with a clearer explanation of its usage.
review_feedback.impact: The PR was closed unmerged; future Transmuter tooling should return with clearer docs and a more complete integration story.

follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that reintroduced Transmuter tooling with fuller documentation.
Search for `transmuter-prepare.sh` or `transmuter-compile.sh` in subsequent commits to see whether this approach was revised or merged elsewhere.
Compare this hardcoded compile wrapper against the current ninja `mwcc_sjis` rule before reusing it, especially MWCC version, include paths, and flags.
If adopting Transmuter workflows, document expected installation path, `TRANSMUTER` environment override, build prerequisites, and examples that do not intentionally break matched source.
